AlgebraicKernelForCircles.name: AlgebraicKernelForCircles
AlgebraicKernelForCircles.brief: The AlgebraicKernelForCircles concept is meant to provide the curved kernel with all the algebraic functionalities required for the manipulation of circular arcs.
AlgebraicKernelForCircles.detailed: Has models CGAL::Algebraic_kernel_for_circles_2_2 CircularKernel CGAL::Circular_kernel_2 <Kernel, AlgebraicKernelForCircles >

AlgebraicKernelForCircles::Polynomial_1_.brief: Concept to represent bivariate polynomials of degree 1 whose coefficients are of a type that is a model of the concept RingNumberType .

AlgebraicKernelForCircles::PolynomialFor.name: AlgebraicKernelForCircles::PolynomialForCircles_2_2
AlgebraicKernelForCircles::PolynomialFor.brief: Concept to represent bivariate polynomials of degree up to 2 capable of storing equations of circles, whose center's coordinates, as well as the square of the radius, are of a type that is a model of the concept FieldNumberType .
AlgebraicKernelForCircles::PolynomialFor.detailed: Refines CopyConstructible Assignable DefaultConstructible Has models CGAL::Polynomial_for_circles_2_2 AlgebraicKernelForCircles

AlgebraicKernelForCircles::RootForCircle.name: AlgebraicKernelForCircles::RootForCircles_2_2
AlgebraicKernelForCircles::RootForCircle.brief: Concept to represent the roots of a system of two equations of degree 2 in two variables x and y that are models of concept AlgebraicKernelForCircles::PolynomialForCircles_2_2 .

CGAL::Circular_arc_2.name: CGAL::Circular_arc_2
CGAL::Circular_arc_2.detailed: Is model of CircularKernel::CircularArc_2 CGAL::Circular_arc_point_2 < CircularKernel > CGAL::Line_arc_2 < CircularKernel >

members.name: Circular_arc_2
members.brief: Constructs an arc from a full circle.

members.name: Circular_arc_2
members.brief: Constructs the circular arc supported by c , whose source is p and whose target is q when traversing the circle in counterclockwise direction.
members.detailed: p and q lie on c .
members.argsstring: (const Circle_2< CircularKernel > &c, const Circular_arc_point_2< CircularKernel > &p, const Circular_arc_point_2< CircularKernel > &q)

members.name: Circular_arc_2
members.brief: Constructs an arc that is supported by the circle of type Circle_2< CircularKernel > passing through the points p , q and r .
members.detailed: The source and target are respectively p and r , when traversing the supporting circle in the counterclockwise direction. Note that, depending on the orientation of the point triple (p,q,r) , q may not lie on the arc. p , q , and r are not collinear.
members.argsstring: (const Point_2< CircularKernel > &p, const Point_2< CircularKernel > &q, const Point_2< CircularKernel > &r)

members.name: center
members.brief: returns the center of the supporting circle.

members.name: squared_radius
members.brief: returns the squared radius of the supporting circle.

members.name: left
members.detailed: ca . is_x_monotone() .

members.name: right
members.detailed: ca . is_x_monotone() .

members.name: bbox
members.brief: Returns a bounding box containing the arc.

members.name: is_x_monotone
members.brief: Tests whether the arc is x-monotone.

members.name: is_y_monotone
members.brief: Tests whether the arc is y-monotone.

members.name: operator==
members.detailed: Two arcs are equal, iff their non-oriented supporting circles are equal (i.e. they have same center and same squared radius) and their endpoints are equal.

members.brief: Returns a bounding box around the point.

members.name: operator==
members.detailed: Two points are equal, iff their x and y coordinates are equal.

members.name: operator<
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically smaller than q , i.e. either if p.x() < q.x() or if p.x() == q.x() and p.y() < q.y() .

members.name: operator>
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically greater than q .

members.name: operator<=
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically smaller than or equal to q .

members.name: operator>=
members.brief: Returns true iff p is lexicographically greater than or equal to q .

CGAL::Circular_kernel_2.name: CGAL::Circular_kernel_2
CGAL::Circular_kernel_2.detailed: Is model of CircularKernel Parameters The first parameter of the circular kernel must be instantiated with a model of the Kernel concept. The Circular_kernel_2 class template derives from this first parameter, in order to reuse all needed functionalities on basic objects provided by a model of the Kernel concept. The second parameter, AlgebraicKernelForCircles , is meant to provide the circular kernel with all the algebraic functionalities required for the manipulation of algebraic curves. Kernel AlgebraicKernelForCircles CGAL::Exact_circular_kernel_2

CGAL::Exact_circular_kernel_2.name: CGAL::Exact_circular_kernel_2
CGAL::Exact_circular_kernel_2.brief: A typedef to a circular kernel that provides both exact geometric predicates and exact geometric constructions.
CGAL::Exact_circular_kernel_2.detailed: This kernel uses some geometric filtering (based on bounding boxes) to gain efficiency. Is model of CircularKernel CGAL::Circular_kernel_2 <Kernel, AlgebraicKernelForCircles >

CGAL::Line_arc_2.name: CGAL::Line_arc_2
CGAL::Line_arc_2.detailed: Is model of CircularKernel::LineArc_2 I/O The format for input/output is, for each line arc: a Line_2 (the supporting line) and two Circular_arc_point_2 (the two endpoints), under the condition that the endpoints are actually lying on the line. CGAL::Circular_arc_point_2 < CircularKernel > CGAL::Circular_arc_2 < CircularKernel >

members.name: Line_arc_2
members.brief: Construct the line segment supported by l , whose source is p1 and whose target is p2 .
members.detailed: p1 and p2 lie on l .
members.argsstring: (const Line_2< CircularKernel > &l, const Circular_arc_point_2< CircularKernel > &p1, const Circular_arc_point_2< CircularKernel > &p2)

members.name: Line_arc_2
members.argsstring: (const Line_2< CircularKernel > &l, const Point_2< CircularKernel > &p1, const Point_2< CircularKernel > &p2)

members.name: bbox
members.brief: Returns a bounding box containing the line segment.

members.name: operator==
members.detailed: Two arcs are equal, iff their non-oriented supporting lines are equal (i.e. they contain the same set of points) and their endpoints are equal.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Constructs the supporting circle of a circular arc.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Constructs a circle from an equation.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Constructs an arc from a full circle.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Construct the circular arc supported by c , whose source is p1 and whose target is p2 when traversing the circle in counterclockwise direction.
members.detailed: p1 and p2 lie on c .

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Constructs the unique circular arc whose supporting circle is c , and whose source is the intersection of c and c1 with index b1 , and whose target is the intersection of c and c2 of index b2 , where intersections are ordered lexicographically, and when traversing the circle in counterclockwise direction.
members.detailed: c intersects both c1 and c2 , and the arc defined by the intersections has non-zero length.
members.argsstring: (const CircularKernel::Circle_2 &c, const CircularKernel::Circle_2 &c1, bool b1, const CircularKernel::Circle_2 &c2, bool b2)

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Same, for intersections defined by lines instead of circles.
members.argsstring: (const CircularKernel::Circle_2 &c, const CircularKernel::Line_2 &l1, bool b1, const CircularKernel::Line_2 &l2, bool b2)

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Constructs an arc that is supported by the circle of type CircularKernel::Circle_2 passing through the points p , q and r .
members.detailed: The source and target are respectively p and r , when traversing the supporting circle in the counterclockwise direction. Note that, depending on the orientation of the point triple (p,q,r) , q may not lie on the arc. p , q , and r are not collinear.

members.brief: Constructs the line segment whose supporting line is l , whose source endpoint is the $ b_1^{th}$ intersection of l with c1 , and whose target endpoint is the $ b_2^{th}$ intersection of l and c2 , where intersections are ordered lexicographically.
members.detailed: l intersects both c1 and c2 , and the arc defined by the intersections has non-zero length.
members.argsstring: (const CircularKernel::Line_2 &l, const CircularKernel::Circle_2 &c1, bool b1, const CircularKernel::Circle_2 &c2, bool b2)

CircularKernel::HasOn_2.name: CircularKernel::HasOn_2
CircularKernel::HasOn_2.brief: To test whether a point lies on a curve.

members.name: operator()
members.brief: Copies in the output iterator the intersection elements between the two objects.
members.detailed: intersections iterates on elements of type CGAL::Object , in lexicographic order. Type_1 and Type_2 can both be either: CircularKernel::Line_2 CircularKernel::Line_arc_2 CircularKernel::Circle_2 CircularKernel::Circular_arc_2 . Depending on the types Type_1 and Type_2 , these elements can be assigned to std::pair< CircularKernel::Circular_arc_point_2 , unsigned> , where the unsigned integer is the multiplicity of the corresponding intersection point between obj_1 and obj_2 , CircularKernel::Circular_arc_2 in case of an overlap of two circular arcs, CircularKernel::Line_arc_2 in case of an overlap of two line segments or CircularKernel::Line_2 or CircularKernel::Circle_2 in case of two equal input lines or circles.

PkgCircularKernel2Ref.name: PkgCircularKernel2Ref
PkgCircularKernel2Ref.detailed: Pedro Machado Manhães de Castro, Sylvain Pion, and Monique Teillaud This package is an extension of the linear CGAL kernel. It offers functionalities on circles, circular arcs and line segments in the plane. Introduced in: CGAL 3.2 BibTeX: cgal:cpt-cgk2-26a License: GPL Windows Demo: Arrangement of Circular Arcs Classified Reference Pages Geometric Concepts CircularKernel Functors
